title: hieracium rupicoliforme subsp. bellacristae
intro: Hieracium rupicoliforme subsp. bellacristae, also known as Bellacristae Hawkweed, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hieracium rupicoliforme subsp. bellacristae include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate a range of health complaints.
